fingerprints: Login pages, titles, or static resources mention Seeyon / 致远互联
signals: Multipart POST returns upload-success markers, file id, or a retrievable relative path
Legacy Seeyon deployment keeps uploaded content under web-accessible servlet paths
verification_summary: POST a harmless multipart probe to /seeyon/htmlofficeservlet and confirm upload-success markers without dropping executable content
exploitation_summary: Upload a JSP foothold through /seeyon/htmlofficeservlet, then trigger it from the returned path

fingerprints: Seeyon WPS or office-integration components are enabled
signals: Servlet accepts multipart office-attachment traffic before full authentication
Response exposes attachment identifiers or relative storage paths
verification_summary: POST a benign office-style multipart probe to /seeyon/wpsAssistServlet and confirm upload handling without writing executable content
exploitation_summary: Abuse /seeyon/wpsAssistServlet to place a controllable server-side payload into a reachable path

fingerprints: Login pages, SPA routes, or static assets mention Ecology / 泛微
signals: BeanShell endpoint returns interpreter output or a recognizable error banner
POSTed bsh.script content changes the HTTP response body
verification_summary: POST a harmless BeanShell print probe to /bsh.servlet.BshServlet and match the echoed marker
exploitation_summary: POST a BeanShell execution script to /bsh.servlet.BshServlet to run id / whoami and pivot from returned output

fingerprints: Static assets or page titles mention e-office / 泛微 e-office
signals: Unauthenticated or weakly protected uploadPicture endpoint accepts multipart data
Response contains attachment id, stored filename, or relative upload path
verification_summary: POST a harmless file probe to /general/index/UploadFile.php?m=uploadPicture&uploadType=eoffice_logo&userId= and confirm saved-path markers
exploitation_summary: Use the uploadPicture flow to place a controllable payload or disguised script in a reachable directory

fingerprints: Login pages or static assets mention NC, UAP, 用友, or nccloud
signals: BeanShell servlet returns script output or execution errors
POSTed bsh.script changes the response body without full authentication
verification_summary: POST a harmless BeanShell print probe to /servlet/~ic/bsh.servlet.BshServlet and match a known marker
exploitation_summary: POST a BeanShell execution script to /servlet/~ic/bsh.servlet.BshServlet and use the command output to enumerate the host

fingerprints: Login pages or resources mention EKP / 蓝凌
signals: custom.jsp reflects server-side file content or template errors when attacker-controlled file paths are supplied
Accessible configuration files reveal JDBC, LDAP, or admin account material
verification_summary: Request /sys/ui/extend/varkind/custom.jsp with a harmless readable target such as login.jsp and match returned page fragments
exploitation_summary: Read WEB-INF or KmssConfig files through custom.jsp, then pivot with harvested credentials, tokens, or database connection data
